# Initialize TypeScript Package Structure

Implements: [[s-9brh|Multi-Agent Git Tracking System]]

## Objective

Set up the foundational TypeScript package with proper structure, dependencies, and tooling.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create package structure:
  ```
  dataplane/
  ├── src/
  │   ├── index.ts              # Public exports
  │   ├── tracker.ts            # MultiAgentRepoTracker main class
  │   ├── models/
  │   │   ├── index.ts
  │   │   ├── stream.ts         # Stream, StreamStatus
  │   │   ├── operation.ts      # Operation, OperationType
  │   │   └── worktree.ts       # AgentWorktree
  │   ├── db/
  │   │   ├── index.ts
  │   │   └── database.ts       # better-sqlite3 wrapper
  │   ├── git/
  │   │   ├── index.ts
  │   │   └── commands.ts       # Git command helpers
  │   └── errors.ts             # Custom error classes
  ├── tests/
  │   ├── setup.ts              # Test fixtures (temp repos, etc.)
  │   ├── tracker.test.ts
  │   └── ...
  ├── package.json
  ├── tsconfig.json
  └── vitest.config.ts
  ```

- [ ] Set up package.json with dependencies:
  - better-sqlite3 (SQLite with WAL)
  - vitest for testing
  - typescript, tsx
  - eslint, prettier

- [ ] Configure tsconfig.json:
  - Strict mode enabled
  - ES2022 target
  - Node16 module resolution

- [ ] Add .gitignore entries for Node/TypeScript

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm install` works
- [ ] `npm run build` compiles without errors
- [ ] `npm test` runs (even with no tests yet)
- [ ] Package imports without errors

## Implementation Complete

Successfully initialized TypeScript package structure with:

**Package Setup**:
- package.json with better-sqlite3, vitest, tsx, typescript
- tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022, NodeNext modules

**Source Structure**:
```
src/
├── index.ts           # Public exports
├── tracker.ts         # MultiAgentRepoTracker stub
├── errors.ts          # All error classes
├── models/
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── stream.ts      # Stream, StreamStatus, CreateStreamOptions
│   ├── operation.ts   # Operation, OperationType
│   ├── worktree.ts    # AgentWorktree
│   └── stack.ts       # StackEntry, ReviewStatus
├── db/
│   ├── index.ts
│   └── database.ts    # SQLite WAL with full schema
└── git/
    ├── index.ts
    └── commands.ts    # Git command helpers
```

**Test Setup**:
- tests/setup.ts with createTestRepo helper
- tests/tracker.test.ts with basic initialization tests

**Verification**:
- `npm install` ✓
- `npm run build` ✓
- `npm test:run` ✓ (3/3 tests pass)

**Note**: Renamed `commit` column to `commit_hash` in stack_entries table to avoid SQLite reserved keyword conflict.

title: Implement stack rebuilding after rebase
content:
# Implement Stack Rebuilding After Rebase

Implements: [[s-443y]]

## Objective

Implement `rebuildStack()` function that updates stack entries after a rebase changes commit hashes.

## Algorithm

1. Get all review blocks for the stack
2. Get current commits in stream (git rev-list base..stream/id)
3. For each stack entry:
   - Compute patch-id of originalCommit
   - Find new commit with matching patch-id
   - Update commitHash to new commit
   - If no match found, delete entry (commit was squashed/dropped)
4. If block has no commits left, delete the block
5. If autoPopulate=true in config, create review blocks for untracked commits

## Functions

- `rebuildStack(streamId, stackName?)` - Main rebuild function
- `autoPopulateUntracked()` - Helper for auto-populating new commits

## Integration

Update rebase operations in `src/streams.ts` to call `rebuildStack()` after successful rebase when `enableStackedReview=true`.

## Tests

- Update commit hashes after rebase
- Delete orphaned entries for squashed commits
- Delete empty review blocks
- Auto-populate new commits when enabled
- Match commits using patch-id

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] rebuildStack() correctly updates commit hashes using patch-id
- [ ] Orphaned entries deleted when commits squashed
- [ ] Empty blocks deleted
- [ ] Auto-populate works when enabled
- [ ] Integration with rebase operations
- [ ] At least 7 new tests passing

- `mergeReviewBlocks(reviewBlockIds[], title, description?)` - Merge multiple blocks into one

### Reordering (Deferred)

content:
# Implement syncWithParent

Implements: [[s-443y]]

## Objective

Implement `syncWithParent()` as a convenience wrapper around `rebaseOntoStream()`.

## Function Signature

```typescript
function syncWithParent(
  streamId: string, 
  agentId: string, 
  worktree: string,
  onConflict?: ConflictStrategy
): RebaseResult;
```

## Behavior

1. Get stream and verify it has a parent
2. Call `rebaseOntoStream()` with parent as target
3. Return result

## Error Cases

- Throw if stream has no parent
- Throw if parent stream doesn't exist

## Tests

- Sync with parent (no conflicts)
- Error when no parent
- Conflict handling passed through

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] syncWithParent() works for streams with parents
- [ ] Proper error when stream has no parent
- [ ] Conflict strategy passed to rebaseOntoStream()

title: Implement dependency tracking with cycle detection
content:
# Implement Dependency Tracking

Implements: [[s-443y]]

## Objective

Implement dependency management functions with cycle detection.

## Functions

```typescript
// Add dependency (validates no cycles)
function addDependency(streamId: string, dependsOnId: string): void;

// Remove dependency
function removeDependency(streamId: string, dependsOnId: string): void;

// Get streams this stream depends on
function getDependencies(streamId: string): string[];

// Get streams that depend on this stream
function getDependents(streamId: string): string[];

// Check if adding dependency would create cycle
function wouldCreateCycle(streamId: string, dependsOnId: string): boolean;
```

## Storage

Use existing `dependencies` table with JSON array in `depends_on` column.

## Cycle Detection Algorithm

Use DFS to detect if adding A → B would create a cycle:
1. Start from B
2. Follow all dependencies recursively
3. If we reach A, adding A → B would create cycle

## Tests

- Add/remove dependencies
- Get dependencies list
- Get dependents list (reverse lookup)
- Cycle detection prevents A → B → A
- Cycle detection prevents A → B → C → A
- Self-dependency prevented

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add/remove dependencies works
- [ ] getDependencies() returns direct dependencies
- [ ] getDependents() returns streams depending on this one
- [ ] Cycles detected and prevented
- [ ] Self-dependencies prevented

title: Implement rebaseOntoStream with conflict strategies
content:
# Implement rebaseOntoStream

Implements: [[s-443y]]

## Objective

Implement `rebaseOntoStream()` function that rebases a source stream's commits onto a target stream's head, with configurable conflict resolution.

## Conflict Strategies

```typescript
type ConflictStrategy = 
  | 'abort'    // Abort rebase, return conflict info (default)
  | 'ours'     // Accept current branch changes
  | 'theirs'   // Accept incoming changes  
  | 'agent'    // Call conflict handler for agent resolution
  | 'manual';  // Fail out, let user resolve manually
```

## Algorithm

1. Get source stream's commits since baseCommit
2. Checkout source stream branch in worktree
3. Run `git rebase --onto <target_head> <base_commit>`
4. Handle conflicts based on strategy
5. Update source stream's `baseCommit` to target's head
6. If `enableStackedReview=true`, call `rebuildStack()` automatically
7. Record operation

## Types to Add

- `ConflictStrategy` type
- `RebaseOptions` interface
- `RebaseResult` interface
- `ConflictInfo` interface

## Tests

- Successful rebase (no conflicts)
- Rebase with abort strategy
- Rebase with ours/theirs strategy
- Auto-rebuild stack after rebase
- Update baseCommit after rebase

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] rebaseOntoStream() works for clean rebases
- [ ] Conflict strategies implemented (abort, ours, theirs, manual)
- [ ] Agent strategy deferred to Phase 6
- [ ] Stack auto-rebuilt when enabled
- [ ] baseCommit updated after successful rebase

title: Add Change types and models
Implement Change lifecycle operations
content:
# Implement Change Lifecycle Operations

Implements: [[s-3a15]]

## Objective

Add functions for squash, split, merge, and drop lifecycle events.

## Functions to Implement

```typescript
// Record squash: multiple changes absorbed into one
recordSquash(
  db: Database,
  absorbedIds: string[],   // Changes being squashed
  targetId: string,         // Change that absorbs them
  resultCommit: string      // New commit hash after squash
): void;

// Record split: one change becomes multiple
recordSplit(
  db: Database,
  originalId: string,
  newCommits: Array<{commit: string, description: string}>
): string[];  // Returns new change IDs

// Mark changes as merged (when stream is merged)
markMerged(db: Database, changeIds: string[]): void;

// Mark change as dropped (commit removed from history)
markDropped(db: Database, changeId: string): void;
```

## Behavior

**Squash**:
- Set absorbed changes status='squashed', squashed_into=targetId
- Update target's current_commit, add to history with reason='squash_target'

**Split**:
- Set original status='dropped', current_commit=null
- Create new changes for each new commit, set split_from=originalId

**Merged**:
- Set status='merged' for all specified changes

**Dropped**:
- Set status='dropped', current_commit=null

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] recordSquash updates all involved changes correctly
- [ ] recordSplit creates new changes with split_from reference
- [ ] markMerged bulk updates status
- [ ] markDropped marks single change as dropped

title: Implement ChangeTracker core CRUD
Integrate Change tracking with commit creation
Integrate Change tracking with rebase operations
content:
# Integrate Change Tracking with Rebase Operations

Implements: [[s-3a15]]

## Objective

Automatically update Change tracking when rebases occur.

## Functions to Implement

```typescript
// Rebuild change tracking after rebase using commit mapping
rebuildChangesAfterRebase(
  db: Database,
  streamId: string,
  commitMapping: Map<string, string>  // old commit -> new commit
): void;

// Build commit mapping from old/new commit lists using patch-id
buildRebaseCommitMapping(
  worktree: string,
  oldCommits: string[],
  newCommits: string[]
): Map<string, string>;
```

## Integration Points

### rebaseOntoStream

After successful rebase:
1. Get old commits (before rebase) from stream's commit range
2. Get new commits (after rebase) from new commit range
3. Build mapping using patch-id
4. Call rebuildChangesAfterRebase

### syncWithParent

Same integration as rebaseOntoStream (it delegates to rebaseOntoStream anyway).

### mergeStream

After successful merge:
1. Get all changes for source stream
2. Call markMerged for all of them

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] buildRebaseCommitMapping correctly maps via patch-id
- [ ] rebuildChangesAfterRebase updates all affected changes
- [ ] rebaseOntoStream calls rebuild automatically
- [ ] mergeStream marks changes as merged

title: Add change_id to stack entries
content:
# Add change_id to Stack Entries

Implements: [[s-3a15]]

## Objective

Add optional change_id column to stack_entries for logical change tracking in reviews.

## Schema Change

Add column to stack_entries:
```sql
ALTER TABLE stack_entries ADD COLUMN change_id TEXT;
CREATE INDEX idx_stack_entries_change ON stack_entries(change_id);
```

Note: Since we use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, need to handle migration for existing DBs or just add the column in schema.

## Code Changes

### 1. Update database.ts schema

Add `change_id TEXT` column to stack_entries table definition.

### 2. Update StackEntry model

```typescript
interface StackEntry {
  // ... existing fields
  changeId?: string;  // Optional Change-Id reference
}
```

### 3. Update rebuildStack

When rebuilding stack after rebase:
1. For each commit, extract Change-Id from message
2. Populate change_id in stack entry

### 4. Add query helper

```typescript
// Get all stack entries for a change across all rebases
getStackEntriesForChange(db, changeId): StackEntry[];
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] change_id column added to schema
- [ ] StackEntry model includes changeId
- [ ] rebuildStack populates change_id from commit message
- [ ] Can query stack entries by change_id

title: Add cascade rebase tests
content:
Create `tests/cascade.test.ts` with comprehensive tests.

**Test cases:**
1. Linear chain cascade (A → B → C)
2. Fan-out cascade (A → B, C, D)
3. Diamond detection throws error
4. Topological sort correctness
5. stop_on_conflict strategy behavior
6. skip_conflicting strategy behavior
7. Worktree provider modes
8. Auto-cascade after rebaseOntoStream
9. Cascade opt-out via option flag

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

feedback.content:
## Phase 5 Implementation Complete

### Files Created/Modified:
- `src/models/dependency.ts` - New types (DependencyType, WorktreeMode, CascadeStrategy, CascadeResult)
- `src/errors.ts` - Added DiamondDependencyError
- `src/dependencies.ts` - Added topologicalSort, getDependencyType, isDiamondDependency, addForkDependency, addMergeDependency
- `src/worktrees.ts` - Added createWorktreeProvider with 3 modes (callback, temporary, sequential)
- `src/cascade.ts` - New cascade rebase logic
- `src/streams.ts` - Updated forkStream to auto-add fork dependency, updated rebaseOntoStream to auto-cascade
- `src/models/stream.ts` - Added cascade option to RebaseOntoStreamOptions, cascadeResult to RebaseResult
- `tests/cascade.test.ts` - 13 new tests

### Test Coverage:
- Linear chain cascade (A → B → C) ✓
- Fan-out cascade (A → B, C, D) ✓
- Diamond detection throws error ✓
- Topological sort correctness ✓
- stop_on_conflict strategy ✓
- skip_conflicting strategy ✓
- Cascade opt-out via cascade=false ✓

### Design Decisions Implemented:
1. **Automatic cascade**: After rebaseOntoStream, cascade triggers automatically (opt-out with cascade=false)
2. **All three worktree modes**: callback, temporary, sequential
3. **Fork dependencies**: Automatically added when forking
4. **Diamond detection**: Throws DiamondDependencyError for manual resolution

Total: 136 tests passing

title: Add DependencyType and error classes
content:
Add type definitions and error classes for cascade rebase.

**Types to add in `src/models/dependency.ts`:**
- `DependencyType = 'fork' | 'merge' | 'rebase_onto'`
- `WorktreeMode = 'callback' | 'temporary' | 'sequential'`
- `WorktreeProviderOptions` interface
- `CascadeStrategy` type
- `CascadeResult` interface

**Errors to add in `src/errors.ts`:**
- `CyclicDependencyError` - thrown when cycle detected
- `DiamondDependencyError` - thrown when merge-based streams detected

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

title: Add automatic cascade trigger to rebaseOntoStream
content:
Update `rebaseOntoStream()` in `src/streams.ts` to automatically trigger cascade.

**Changes:**
- Add optional `cascade` parameter to `RebaseOntoStreamOptions`:
  - `cascade?: boolean | CascadeOptions` (default: true)
  - `CascadeOptions = { strategy?: CascadeStrategy; worktree?: WorktreeProviderOptions }`
- After successful rebase, if cascade enabled:
  - Call `cascadeRebase()` for all dependents
  - Return combined result including cascade results

**Default behavior:**
- Use `sequential` worktree mode with the same worktree path
- Use `stop_on_conflict` strategy

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

title: Implement topological sort in dependencies.ts
content:
Add topological sort using Kahn's algorithm to `src/dependencies.ts`.

**Functions to add:**
- `topologicalSort(db, streamIds: string[]): string[]` - sort streams so dependencies come before dependents
- `getDependencyType(db, streamId): DependencyType | null` - get how stream depends on parents
- `isDiamondDependency(db, streamId): boolean` - detect if stream has multiple parents

**Update existing:**
- Update `addDependency` to accept optional `dependencyType` parameter
- Add `addForkDependency(db, child, parent)` helper
- Add `addMergeDependency(db, target, sources[])` helper

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

title: Add worktree provider utilities
content:
Add utilities for the three worktree modes in `src/worktrees.ts` or extend existing.

**Modes to support:**
1. **callback**: User-provided `(streamId) => worktreePath` function
2. **temporary**: Auto-create temp worktrees, clean up after cascade
3. **sequential**: Reuse single worktree, checkout each stream in turn

**Functions needed:**
- `createWorktreeProvider(options: WorktreeProviderOptions): WorktreeProvider`
- `WorktreeProvider.getWorktree(streamId): string`
- `WorktreeProvider.cleanup(): void` - for temporary mode

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

title: Auto-add fork dependency in forkStream
content:
Update `forkStream()` in `src/streams.ts` to automatically add a fork dependency.

**Changes:**
- After creating the forked stream, call `addForkDependency(db, newStreamId, parentStreamId)`
- This ensures the dependency graph stays in sync with parent_stream relationships

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

title: Implement cascade rebase logic
content:
Implement the core cascade rebase algorithm in `src/cascade.ts`.

**CascadeRebase class or functions:**
- `cascadeRebase(db, repoPath, options): CascadeResult`

**Algorithm:**
1. Get all dependents of root stream via `getAllDependents`
2. Sort topologically via `topologicalSort`
3. For each stream in order:
   - Check if any dependency failed → skip if so
   - Check for diamond dependency → throw `DiamondDependencyError`
   - Get worktree via provider
   - Call `rebaseOntoStream` for the stream
   - Handle result based on strategy

**Strategies:**
- `stop_on_conflict`: Stop entire cascade at first conflict
- `skip_conflicting`: Mark as failed, continue with others

Implements [[s-2u7i]]

title: Implement agent conflict handler in rebaseOntoStream
content:
Implement the `onConflict: 'agent'` strategy in `rebaseOntoStream()`.

**Tasks:**
1. Add `conflictTimeout` option (default 300000ms = 5 min)

2. When conflict detected and `onConflict === 'agent'`:
   a. Create conflict record (status: 'pending')
   b. Set stream status to 'conflicted'
   c. If `conflictHandler` provided:
      - Update conflict status to 'in_progress'
      - Call handler with timeout wrapper
      - If success: verify resolution, continue rebase
      - If failure: abort rebase, set status 'abandoned'
   d. If no handler: return with conflict info

3. Add timeout wrapper for handler:
   ```typescript
   async function withTimeout<T>(
     promise: Promise<T>,
     ms: number
   ): Promise<T>
   ```

4. Add `ConflictResolutionError` to errors.ts

5. Update `RebaseResult` to include conflict record ID when applicable

Implements [[s-8r5w]]

feedback.content:
## Implementation Complete

Implemented agent conflict handler in rebaseOntoStream:

1. **New options** in `RebaseOntoStreamOptions`:
   - `conflictTimeout` - timeout in ms (default 5 min)

2. **Updated `RebaseResult`**:
   - Added `conflictId` field

3. **Helper function**:
   - `withTimeout()` - exported async timeout wrapper

4. **Agent handler flow** (`handleAgentConflictResolution`):
   - Tries rebase, if success returns immediately
   - On conflict: creates conflict record, sets stream to 'conflicted'
   - If no handler: aborts rebase, returns with conflictId for deferred resolution
   - If handler provided:
     - Calls handler with timeout
     - On success: verifies resolution, stages, continues rebase
     - On failure: aborts, abandons conflict, keeps stream conflicted
   - Handles partial resolution (rolls back)

5. **Note**: The handler execution uses a sync-compatible busy-wait pattern since `rebaseOntoStream` is synchronous. For production, this should be refactored to async.

All 244 tests pass.

title: Add 'conflicted' stream status and blocking
content:
Add `'conflicted'` to `StreamStatus` type and implement blocking logic.

**Tasks:**
1. Update `StreamStatus` type in `models/stream.ts`:
   ```typescript
   type StreamStatus = 'active' | 'merged' | 'archived' | 'conflicted';
   ```

2. Add `setStreamConflicted(db, streamId)` function in `streams.ts`

3. Add guard checks in mutation functions to block operations on conflicted streams:
   - `commitChanges()` - block
   - `rebaseOntoStream()` - block (except resolution)
   - `mergeStream()` - block
   - `syncWithParent()` - block

4. Add `StreamConflictedError` to `errors.ts`

5. Allow these operations on conflicted streams:
   - Read operations (getStream, etc.)
   - Conflict resolution functions
   - Rollback operations

Implements [[s-8r5w]]

feedback.content:
## Implementation Complete

Added 'conflicted' stream status and blocking:

1. **StreamStatus type** - Added `'conflicted'` to `models/stream.ts`

2. **New error classes** in `errors.ts`:
   - `StreamConflictedError` - thrown when blocked operation attempted
   - `ConflictResolutionError` - for resolution failures (timeout, handler_failed, etc.)

3. **Stream status functions** in `streams.ts`:
   - `setStreamConflicted(db, streamId, conflictId?)` - marks stream as conflicted
   - `clearStreamConflicted(db, streamId)` - resets to active
   - `assertStreamNotConflicted(stream)` - internal guard helper

4. **Blocking guards** added to:
   - `mergeStream()` - blocks both source and target if conflicted
   - `rebaseOntoStream()` - blocks source stream if conflicted
   - `syncWithParent()` - blocks if stream conflicted
   - `commitChanges()` - blocks if stream conflicted

All 244 tests pass.

title: Implement conflict resolution and rebase continuation
content:
Implement functions to resolve conflicts and continue rebases.

**Tasks:**
1. Add to `conflicts.ts`:
   ```typescript
   // Start resolution (for tracking)
   startConflictResolution(db, conflictId, agentId): void
   
   // Mark as resolved after files are fixed
   markConflictResolved(db, conflictId, resolution): void
   ```

2. Add git helpers to `git/commands.ts`:
   ```typescript
   // Check if rebase is in progress
   isRebaseInProgress(options): boolean
   
   // Continue rebase after resolution
   rebaseContinue(options): RebaseOntoResult
   
   // Get rebase state info
   getRebaseState(options): { onto: string, head: string } | null
   ```

3. Add to `streams.ts`:
   ```typescript
   // Continue a paused rebase after conflict resolution
   continueRebase(db, repoPath, streamId, worktree, resolution): RebaseResult
   ```

4. Handle partial resolution (some files resolved):
   - If `git rebase --continue` fails, roll back
   - Set conflict to 'abandoned'
   - Keep stream 'conflicted'

Implements [[s-8r5w]]

feedback.content:
Implemented conflict resolution and rebase continuation:

**Git helpers (git/commands.ts):**
- `isRebaseInProgress()` - Checks for rebase-merge or rebase-apply directories
- `getRebaseState()` - Returns onto, head, step, and total for progress tracking
- `rebaseContinue()` - Continues rebase and returns `RebaseOntoResult` with conflict handling

**Stream operations (streams.ts):**
- `continueRebase(db, repoPath, streamId, worktree, resolution)` - Continues a paused rebase after manual conflict resolution
  - Verifies stream is in 'conflicted' state
  - Verifies rebase is actually in progress
  - Checks for remaining unresolved conflicts
  - Stages resolved files and continues rebase
  - Updates conflict record with resolution details
  - Clears stream's conflicted status on success
  
- `abortConflictedRebase(db, repoPath, streamId, worktree)` - Aborts a conflicted rebase
  - Runs git rebase --abort
  - Abandons the conflict record
  - Reverts stream to 'active' status

**Partial resolution handling:**
- If `git rebase --continue` fails with more conflicts, returns the new conflict list
- Stream remains 'conflicted' until all conflicts resolved
- Conflict record tracks resolution progress

title: Add defer_conflicts cascade strategy
content:
Implement the `defer_conflicts` cascade strategy.

**Tasks:**
1. Add to `CascadeStrategy` type:
   ```typescript
   type CascadeStrategy =
     | 'stop_on_conflict'
     | 'skip_conflicting'
     | 'defer_conflicts';
   ```

2. Update `CascadeRebaseOptions`:
   ```typescript
   interface CascadeRebaseOptions {
     // ... existing
     conflictHandlers?: Map<string, ConflictHandler>;  // Per-stream
     conflictTimeout?: number;
   }
   ```

3. Update `CascadeResult`:
   ```typescript
   interface CascadeResult {
     // ... existing
     deferred: string[];  // Stream IDs with deferred conflicts
     conflictRecords: Record<string, string>;  // streamId -> conflictId
   }
   ```

4. Implement `defer_conflicts` in `cascadeRebase()`:
   - On conflict: record it, mark stream conflicted
   - Skip dependents of conflicted streams
   - Continue with other streams
   - Return deferred list

5. Add `resumeCascade()` function:
   - Check all previously deferred streams
   - If resolved, continue cascade for their dependents

Implements [[s-8r5w]]

feedback.content:
Implemented defer_conflicts cascade strategy:

**Model updates (models/dependency.ts):**
- Added `'defer_conflicts'` to `CascadeStrategy` type
- Added `conflictHandlers?: Map<string, ConflictHandler>` to `CascadeOptions` for per-stream handlers
- Added `conflictTimeout?: number` to `CascadeOptions`
- Added `deferred?: string[]` to `CascadeResult` for streams with deferred conflicts
- Added `conflictRecords?: Record<string, string>` to `CascadeResult` mapping stream IDs to conflict record IDs

**Cascade implementation (cascade.ts):**
- Updated `CascadeRebaseOptions` with `conflictHandlers` and `conflictTimeout`
- Updated `cascadeRebase()` to:
  - Track deferred streams separately from failed
  - Skip dependents of deferred (conflicted) streams
  - Pass per-stream conflict handlers to rebaseOntoStream
  - Use 'agent' conflict strategy when handler provided
  - Record conflict IDs in result

**New `resumeCascade()` function:**
- Takes previously deferred stream IDs
- Checks which are now resolved (no longer conflicted)
- Cascades to dependents of resolved streams
- Continues to use defer_conflicts for any new conflicts
- Returns combined deferred list (still conflicted + newly deferred)

title: Implement conflict record CRUD operations
content:
Implement conflict persistence using the existing `conflicts` table.

Created conflict record CRUD operations:

2. **Types** (`ConflictRecord`, `ConflictStatus`, `ConflictResolution`, etc.)

All 244 tests pass.

title: Add conflict handling tests
content:
Comprehensive tests for Phase 6 conflict handling.

**Test files:**
- `tests/conflicts.test.ts` - Conflict CRUD and lifecycle
- `tests/conflict-resolution.test.ts` - Agent handler and resolution

**Test cases:**

1. **Conflict Record CRUD**:
   - Create and retrieve conflict
   - Update conflict status
   - Resolve conflict with resolution details
   - Abandon conflict

2. **Stream Blocking**:
   - Block commits on conflicted stream
   - Block rebase on conflicted stream
   - Allow reads on conflicted stream
   - Allow resolution operations

3. **Agent Handler**:
   - Handler resolves conflict successfully
   - Handler fails, conflict abandoned
   - Handler timeout
   - No handler provided, conflict deferred

4. **Cascade with Conflicts**:
   - stop_on_conflict stops everything
   - skip_conflicting continues others
   - defer_conflicts records and continues
   - Per-stream handlers

5. **Resolution Flow**:
   - Continue rebase after resolution
   - Partial resolution rolls back
   - Clear conflict resets stream

6. **Recovery**:
   - Orphaned in_progress conflicts recovered
   - Stale conflict detection

Implements [[s-8r5w]]

feedback.content:
## Conflict Handling Tests (i-2yn5) - Implementation Complete

### Tests Created

1. **tests/conflicts.test.ts** - 16 tests covering:
   - Conflict record CRUD (create, get, list, update status)
   - Stream blocking when conflicted (blocks writes, allows reads)
   - Worktree operations unaffected by conflict status
   - Stale conflict detection with threshold
   - Orphaned conflict recovery on tracker initialization

2. **tests/conflict-resolution.test.ts** - 7 tests covering:
   - Conflict detection with abort strategy (sets stream to 'conflicted')
   - Auto-resolution with 'ours' strategy (keeps source changes)
   - Auto-resolution with 'theirs' strategy (keeps target changes)
   - Agent handler timeout behavior (marks conflict as abandoned)
   - Fallback to abort when no handler provided
   - Abort and reset of conflicted rebase
   - Complete conflict clearing (deletes record, not just abandons)

### Bug Fix During Testing
- **Git rebase strategy inversion**: Fixed `rebaseOntoWithStrategy` where 'ours' and 'theirs' were swapped. In git rebase semantics, 'ours' refers to the target branch, not the source. Updated to correctly invert the strategies so:
  - API 'ours' → git '--strategy-option=theirs' (keeps source/feature changes)
  - API 'theirs' → git '--strategy-option=ours' (keeps target/main changes)

### Test Results
All 267 tests pass across 17 test files.

title: Add conflict recovery and cleanup
content:
Implement crash recovery and cleanup for orphaned conflicts.

**Tasks:**
1. Add recovery function:
   ```typescript
   // Called on tracker initialization
   recoverOrphanedConflicts(db, repoPath): void
   ```
   - Find conflicts with 'in_progress' status
   - Check if git rebase is still in progress
   - If yes: abort it
   - Set conflict to 'abandoned'

2. Add cleanup for abandoned conflicts:
   ```typescript
   // User decides to start fresh
   clearConflict(db, repoPath, streamId): void
   ```
   - Abort any in-progress rebase
   - Delete conflict record
   - Reset stream status to 'active'
   - Roll back to pre-conflict state

3. Add to `MultiAgentRepoTracker` constructor:
   - Call `recoverOrphanedConflicts()` on init

4. Add conflict age query for monitoring:
   ```typescript
   getStaleConflicts(db, thresholdMs): ConflictRecord[]
   ```

Implements [[s-8r5w]]

feedback.content:
Implemented conflict recovery and cleanup:

**New functions in streams.ts:**

1. `clearConflict(db, streamId, worktree?)` - Completely clear a conflict:
   - Aborts git rebase if worktree provided and rebase in progress
   - Deletes conflict record (not just abandons)
   - Resets stream status to 'active'
   - Use when user wants to start fresh

2. `recoverOrphanedConflicts(db, thresholdMs = 1 hour)` - Crash recovery:
   - Finds conflicts stuck in 'in_progress' status older than threshold
   - Marks them as 'abandoned'
   - Resets associated streams from 'conflicted' to 'active'
   - Returns list of recovered conflict IDs and cleaned stream IDs

**Tracker integration:**
- Added `streams.recoverOrphanedConflicts(this.db)` call in `MultiAgentRepoTracker` constructor
- Automatically cleans up stale conflicts on tracker initialization

**Note on `getStaleConflicts`:**
- Already implemented in conflicts.ts (from i-8pdn)
- Used by recoverOrphanedConflicts for finding orphaned conflicts

Add GC configuration table and defaults


```typescript
}
```

Implements [[s-1gg8]]

title: Add working copy snapshot CRUD
Add safe operation wrappers with auto-snapshot

feedback.content:
## Implementation Complete: Safe Operation Wrappers

### Requirements Met
- `SafeOperationResult<T>` interface implemented with all specified fields: `success`, `result`, `error`, `snapshotId`
- `safeOperation<T>()` sync function implemented as specified
- `safeOperationAsync<T>()` async function implemented as specified

### Behavior Implemented
1. Creates snapshot before operation (if uncommitted changes exist)
2. Executes the provided function
3. On success: returns `{ success: true, result, snapshotId }`
4. On failure: returns `{ success: false, error, snapshotId }` for recovery

### Implementation Details
- Snapshot reason is prefixed with `pre-` (e.g., `pre-rebase`)
- Non-Error throws are converted to Error objects
- snapshotId is `undefined` when no uncommitted changes existed

### JSDoc Examples
Both functions include comprehensive JSDoc with usage examples showing:
- How to wrap risky operations like rebase/merge
- How to check success and handle errors
- How to use snapshotId for recovery with `restore()`

### Test Coverage (18 new tests)
- **safeOperation()**: 7 tests covering success, failure, snapshotId presence/absence, error conversion, generic types
- **safeOperationAsync()**: 9 tests covering async success, rejection, Promise.reject, error conversion, generic types
- **Recovery workflow**: 2 tests demonstrating end-to-end recovery after failed operations

### Evidence
- Build passes: `npm run build`
- All 358 tests pass (including 18 new tests for safe operation wrappers)

anchor.context_before: ention, snapshots should be restored or committed.

title: Add stream guard operations (optimistic concurrency)

feedback.content:
## Implementation Complete: Stream Guards (Optimistic Concurrency)

### Requirements Met
- All 5 functions implemented as specified: `touchGuard`, `validateGuard`, `clearGuard`, `getGuard`, `listActiveGuards`
- `StreamGuard` interface matches spec exactly
- `ConcurrentModificationError` added to errors.ts with streamId, lastWriter, lastWriteTime properties

### Design Decisions
1. **Validation logic**: validateGuard returns true if:
   - No guard exists (safe to proceed)
   - Same agent was last writer (own writes don't conflict)
   - Last write was at or before the read timestamp (concurrent but non-conflicting)
2. **Upsert behavior**: touchGuard uses INSERT OR REPLACE for atomic upsert
3. **listActiveGuards**: Returns guards ordered by lastWrite DESC (most recent first)

### Test Coverage (22 tests)
- touchGuard: create, update, multiple streams
- getGuard: null for non-existent, correct field values
- validateGuard: 5 scenarios including same agent, before/after timestamp
- clearGuard: remove, no-throw on missing, isolation
- listActiveGuards: empty, within window, ordering
- ConcurrentModificationError: properties and throwability
- Usage patterns: concurrent modification detection, guard lifecycle

### Evidence
- Build passes: `npm run build`
- All 315 tests pass including 22 new guards tests

anchor.context_before: efault, configurable | Safety without forcing it |
anchor.context_after: Guards track "who wrote last" to detect concurrent

title: Add archiveStream with auto-archive hooks
Add prune and gc functions

feedback.content:
## Implementation Complete: prune() and gc() functions

### What was implemented:

**`prune()` function (src/gc.ts:407-483):**
- Deletes archived streams older than specified threshold (defaults to `archiveRetentionDays` from config)
- Removes git branches when `deleteGitBranches` config is true
- Cleans up related records: operations, dependencies, conflicts, changes, checkpoints, review blocks, stack configs
- Uses transaction for atomic database deletions
- Returns `PruneResult` with counts and any errors

**`gc()` function (src/gc.ts:500-632):**
Full garbage collection pipeline with 6 steps:
1. Archive merged/abandoned streams (respects auto-archive config)
2. Prune archived streams past retention period
3. Clean up orphaned worktrees via `git worktree prune`
4. Prune old snapshots (7 days retention)
5. Clear incomplete operation checkpoints
6. Release stale locks (>1 hour old)

Returns `GCResult` with detailed summary of all actions taken.

### Design decisions:
- Errors are accumulated but don't stop the GC process (graceful degradation)
- Branch deletion errors for "not found" are silently ignored (branch may already be deleted)
- Uses existing `snapshots.pruneSnapshots()` and `recovery` module functions
- 20 new tests added covering all functionality

### Test coverage:
- All 397 tests passing
- Covered: empty state, single/multiple streams, branch deletion (enabled/disabled), error handling, stale locks, incomplete checkpoints, full pipeline

title: Add health check and startup recovery

feedback.content:
## Implementation Complete: Health Check and Startup Recovery

### What was implemented:

**`healthCheck()` function (src/recovery.ts:269-330):**
- Counts active streams in the database
- Counts archived streams via `gc.listArchivedStreams()`
- Counts active agents via `guards.listActiveGuards()` (60s threshold)
- Finds stale locks older than 5 minutes
- Finds incomplete operations via `getIncompleteCheckpoints()`
- Finds orphaned conflicts via `conflicts.getStaleConflicts()`
- Counts pending snapshots via `snapshots.listSnapshots()`
- Returns `HealthCheckResult` with `healthy` boolean, issues array, and all counts

**`startupRecovery()` function (src/recovery.ts:366-429):**
Three-step recovery process:
1. Clear incomplete checkpoints (cannot git reset without worktree info)
2. Release stale locks older than 5 minutes
3. Recover orphaned conflicts via `streams.recoverOrphanedConflicts()` (1 hour threshold)

Returns `StartupRecoveryResult` with counts and detailed log of actions.

**TrackerOptions updates (src/tracker.ts:50-62):**
- Added `skipRecovery?: boolean` option
- When not skipped, checks `runRecoveryOnStartup` from GCConfig
- Calls `startupRecovery()` in constructor if both conditions are met

**Tracker.healthCheck() method (src/tracker.ts:481-483):**
- Exposes health check via the tracker instance for easy access

### Design decisions:
- 5-minute threshold for stale locks (matches spec)
- 60-second threshold for active agents
- 1-hour threshold for orphaned conflicts (matches existing recoverOrphanedConflicts)
- startupRecovery returns a detailed log for debugging/auditing
- healthCheck returns issues array describing each problem found

### Test coverage (33 new tests):
- healthCheck: healthy state, stream counts, archived counts, active agents, stale locks, incomplete ops, orphaned conflicts, pending snapshots, multiple issues
- startupRecovery: empty result, checkpoint clearing, stale lock release, orphaned conflict recovery, multiple actions
- skipRecovery option: respects skipRecovery flag, respects config runRecoveryOnStartup

### Evidence:
- Build passes: `npm run build`
- All 417 tests pass including 33 new tests for health check and recovery

title: Add Phase 7 tests
content:
Comprehensive tests for Phase 7 safety and GC features.

**Test files to create:**

### tests/gc.test.ts
- GC config CRUD (get defaults, set partial, persist)
- archiveStream moves to archived_streams
- Auto-archive on merge (when enabled)
- Auto-archive on abandon (when enabled)
- No auto-archive when disabled
- prune deletes old archives
- prune removes git branches
- gc runs full pipeline
- gc respects config options

### tests/guards.test.ts
- touchGuard creates/updates guard
- getGuard returns current state
- validateGuard succeeds when no changes
- validateGuard fails when another agent wrote
- validateGuard fails when same agent wrote later
- clearGuard removes guard
- listActiveGuards filters by time
- ConcurrentModificationError has correct info

### tests/snapshots.test.ts
- snapshot creates stash and record
- snapshot returns null when nothing to snapshot
- restore applies stash
- listSnapshots filters by worktree
- listSnapshots filters by agentId
- deleteSnapshot removes record
- pruneSnapshots removes old records
- safeOperation snapshots before execution
- safeOperation returns snapshotId on failure

### tests/recovery.test.ts
- checkpoint creates record
- completeCheckpoint removes record
- getIncompleteCheckpoints finds incomplete ops
- recoverCheckpoint resets to before_state
- healthCheck reports all issues
- startupRecovery fixes issues
- Tracker runs recovery on startup (when configured)
- Tracker skips recovery (when skipRecovery=true)

**Tasks:**
- [ ] Create tests/gc.test.ts
- [ ] Create tests/guards.test.ts
- [ ] Create tests/snapshots.test.ts
- [ ] Create tests/recovery.test.ts
- [ ] Ensure all tests pass

Implements [[s-1gg8]]

feedback.content:
## Phase 7 (Safety & GC) Implementation Complete

All Phase 7 features have been implemented and tested:

### Features Implemented

1. **GC Configuration** (i-6u4g)
   - Database-stored configuration with sensible defaults
   - Settings: autoArchiveOnMerge, autoArchiveOnAbandon, archiveRetentionDays, deleteGitBranches, deleteWorktrees, runRecoveryOnStartup

2. **Operation Checkpoints** (i-500d)
   - Crash recovery checkpoints for multi-step operations
   - CRUD operations: checkpoint(), getCheckpoint(), clearCheckpoint(), listIncompleteCheckpoints()

3. **Stream Guards** (i-97k3)
   - Optimistic concurrency control (detect, don't block)
   - Operations: touchGuard(), validateGuard(), clearGuard(), getGuard(), listActiveGuards()
   - ConcurrentModificationError for detection

4. **Working Copy Snapshots** (i-2pnz)
   - Git stash-based protection for uncommitted work
   - CRUD: snapshot(), restore(), getSnapshot(), deleteSnapshot(), listSnapshots()
   - 90-day git gc limitation documented

5. **Safe Operation Wrappers** (i-8jk9)
   - Auto-snapshot before risky operations
   - safeOperation() and safeOperationAsync() wrappers
   - Automatic cleanup on success, preserved on failure

6. **Archive Stream** (i-49kg)
   - Stream archiving to archived_streams table
   - Auto-archive hooks in updateStreamStatus() (configurable)
   - Transaction-wrapped for atomicity

7. **Prune and GC** (i-66su)
   - prune() removes old archived streams
   - gc() runs full cleanup pipeline
   - Configurable retention periods

8. **Health Check & Startup Recovery** (i-5h61)
   - healthCheck() returns comprehensive system diagnostics
   - startupRecovery() cleans stale state automatically
   - Tracker integration with skipRecovery option

### Test Coverage

- 150 new tests added (267 → 417 total)
- All test files created: gc.test.ts, guards.test.ts, snapshots.test.ts, recovery.test.ts
- All 417 tests passing

### Design Decisions

- Guards use optimistic concurrency (detect concurrent writes without blocking)
- Auto-archive enabled by default, configurable per-behavior
- Startup recovery runs automatically, can be disabled via config or skipRecovery option

title: Phase 1: Database schema and models for worker tasks
content:
Add the database tables and TypeScript interfaces for worker tasks.

## Tasks

1. **Add tables to `src/db/database.ts`**
   - Add `worker_tasks` table with all columns
   - Add `task_merges` table for merge history
   - Add `head_commit` column to streams table
   - Add indexes for efficient queries

2. **Update `src/db/tables.ts`**
   - Add `worker_tasks` to TableNames interface
   - Add `task_merges` to TableNames interface
   - Update `getTables()` function

3. **Create `src/models/task.ts`**
   - Define `WorkerTaskStatus` type
   - Define `WorkerTask` interface
   - Define `TaskMerge` interface
   - Define option interfaces: `CreateTaskOptions`, `StartTaskOptions`, `CompleteTaskOptions`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Database tables created on initialization
- [ ] TypeScript interfaces compile without errors
- [ ] Tables have proper foreign key constraints
- [ ] Indexes created for common query patterns

title: Phase 2: Worker tasks lifecycle module
content:
Create `src/worker-tasks.ts` with full task lifecycle management.

## Functions to Implement

### CRUD Operations
- `createTask(db, options)` - Create task under stream
- `getTask(db, id)` - Get task by ID
- `getTaskOrThrow(db, id)` - Get task or throw if not found
- `listTasks(db, streamId, options?)` - List tasks with filters
- `updateTask(db, id, updates)` - Internal update helper

### Lifecycle Operations
- `startTask(db, repoPath, options)` - Assign agent, create ephemeral branch
- `completeTask(db, repoPath, options)` - Merge to stream with --no-ff
- `abandonTask(db, repoPath, taskId)` - Mark abandoned, cleanup branch
- `releaseTask(db, taskId)` - Release back to open state

### Maintenance Operations
- `cleanupWorkerBranches(db, repoPath, options?)` - GC old branches
- `recoverStaleTasks(db, thresholdMs?)` - Recover stuck in_progress tasks

### Helper Functions
- `generateWorkerBranchName(agentId, taskId)` - Generate branch name
- `recordTaskMerge(db, options)` - Record merge in task_merges table

## Key Implementation Details

- Branch naming: `worker/{agentId}/{taskId}@{timestamp}`
- Merge with `--no-ff` to always create merge commit
- Update stream HEAD after merge
- Track all merges in task_merges for audit

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All CRUD operations work correctly
- [ ] startTask creates branch from stream HEAD
- [ ] completeTask merges with preserved history
- [ ] Cleanup removes old completed/abandoned branches
- [ ] Recovery releases stuck tasks

Implements [[s-24oy]]

title: Phase 4: Diff stack integration for streams
content:
Add functions to `src/diff-stacks.ts` for stream-based checkpoint creation and cherry-pick workflow.

## Functions to Add

### Checkpoint Creation
```typescript
// Create checkpoints from stream commits
createCheckpointsFromStream(
  db, repoPath, streamId: string,
  options?: { from?: string; to?: string }
): Checkpoint[]
```

### Stack Creation
```typescript
// Create diff stack from stream for incremental review
createStackFromStream(db, repoPath, options: {
  streamId: string;
  name?: string;
  description?: string;
  targetBranch?: string;
  commitRange?: { from: string; to: string };
}): DiffStackWithCheckpoints
```

### Cherry-Pick Landing
```typescript
// Cherry-pick approved stack to target branch
cherryPickStackToTarget(
  db, repoPath, stackId: string, worktree: string
): { cherryPickedCommits: string[]; newCommits: string[] }
```

## Implementation Details

1. **createCheckpointsFromStream**
   - Get commits from stream base to HEAD (or specified range)
   - Create checkpoint for each commit
   - Reuse existing checkpoints if commit already tracked

2. **createStackFromStream**
   - Call createCheckpointsFromStream
   - Create diff stack with checkpoints
   - Set target branch (default: stream's base branch)

3. **cherryPickStackToTarget**
   - Verify stack is approved
   - Checkout target branch
   - Cherry-pick each checkpoint in order
   - Update stack status to merged

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Checkpoints created from stream commits
- [ ] Stack groups checkpoints for review
- [ ] Cherry-pick lands commits on target
- [ ] Stack marked as merged after landing

Implements [[s-24oy]]

title: Phase 6: Tests for worker tasks
content:
Write comprehensive tests for the worker tasks feature.

## Test Files to Create

- listTasks filters by status and agent

**Lifecycle Tests**

- startTask assigns agent and updates timestamps
- completeTask merges with --no-ff (verify merge commit)
- completeTask preserves worker commit history
- abandonTask marks task and deletes branch
- releaseTask resets to open status

**Cleanup Tests**

- cleanupWorkerBranches removes orphaned branches
- cleanupWorkerBranches respects threshold

**Recovery Tests**

- recoverStaleTasks preserves branch for recovery

### `tests/stream-hierarchy.test.ts`

- getStreamHierarchy returns tree structure
- Handles multiple levels of nesting
- Includes active tasks per stream

### `tests/integration/worker-tasks-e2e.test.ts`

Full workflow test:
1. Create stream
2. Create tasks
3. Start tasks (multiple agents)
4. Complete tasks
5. Verify merge history preserved
6. Create diff stack
7. Cherry-pick to main

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E workflow test passes
- [ ] Edge cases covered (conflicts, recovery)
- [ ] Test coverage > 80% for new code

title: Phase 5: Tracker API and exports
Phase 3: Stream hierarchy query
content:
Add `getStreamHierarchy` function to `src/streams.ts` for recursive tree queries.

## Functions to Add

```typescript
interface StreamNode {
  stream: Stream;
  children: StreamNode[];
  tasks: WorkerTask[];  // Active tasks on this stream
}

// Get recursive tree of streams
getStreamHierarchy(db, rootId?: string): StreamNode[]
```

## Implementation

1. Query all streams (or subtree from rootId)
2. Build parent-child relationships using `parentStream` field
3. For each stream, include active worker tasks
4. Return tree structure

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Returns correct tree structure
- [ ] Handles unlimited depth recursion
- [ ] Includes active tasks per stream
- [ ] Works with rootId filter or returns all roots

Implements [[s-24oy]]
